description_long: This is a standard hand screwdriver featuring a long, slender metal shaft with a pointed tip and a robust, contoured handle. The handle is primarily red with black rubberized grip inserts, designed for ergonomic comfort and slip resistance during use. The shaft appears to be made of steel, showing a metallic sheen and a straight, uniform profile. The tip is a Phillips type, characterized by its cross-shaped geometry, suitable for driving screws with corresponding heads. The handle shows some visible signs of wear, including minor scuffs and discoloration, indicating regular use. No brand markings or logos are visible on the handle or shaft.
description: A red and black contoured-handled Phillips screwdriver with a steel shaft and visible signs of wear. The handle is ergonomically designed with rubberized grips for comfort and control.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the screwdriver, highlighting the red and black contoured handle and the straight steel shaft with a Phillips tip.
description_view_1: Top-down view focusing on the handle's oval shape and the black rubberized grip inserts, with the shaft appearing as a thin line.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other side of the handle and shaft, with the same color and grip features.
description_view_3: End-on view of the handle, emphasizing the circular cross-section and the alignment of the shaft extending outward.
attribute_handle_type: contoured, plastic handle with black rubberized grip inserts for ergonomic comfort
attribute_visible_wear_or_damage: Minor scuffs and discoloration on the handle
